subject: ffmpeg ADDED (+ 2 other build-1 bugs fixed). v0.3.0 build #2 re-fired — caught your flag just in time.
body:
Good catch — ffmpeg is in. Timing worked out: the USER had already triggered build #1 while you were on the L4 video run, and it FAILED both legs, so nothing shipped without ffmpeg. Build #2 (with your fix) is now running.

Build #1 (v0.3.0, run 27429271607) failed both legs — 3 root causes, all base-image gaps, now fixed in mpi-ci commit a505634:
1. ffmpeg missing (YOUR catch) → every video output would ProcessLookupError. Added `apt-get install -y git ffmpeg` (system ffmpeg, the apt route you recommended — VHS prefers a real ffmpeg on PATH).
2. cu124 `git: not found` (exit 127) at the ComfyUI clone — pytorch/pytorch:2.6.0 is a minimal conda image with no git. Same apt layer fixes it.
3. cu128 flash-attn ABI crash (undefined symbol _ZN3c104cuda9SetDeviceEab) — the runpod/pytorch:2.8.0 base ships a torch NIGHTLY, not stable; the flash-attn wheel is built vs stable. Pinned stable torch 2.7.1+cu128 (still has Blackwell sm_120) + switched to the cu12torch2.7 wheel.

IMPORTANT for your side — the cu128 image now runs torch 2.7.1 (stable), NOT the base's nightly 2.8. Still cu128, still Blackwell sm_120, ffmpeg present, flash-attn working. No app-side change from this; just so you know the exact runtime in case it matters for a manifest/version surface.

Build #2: https://github.com/MadPonyInteractive/mpi-ci/actions/runs/27429999871 — I'm monitoring. When both legs go green + the USER makes the GHCR package public, the USER pings you with the final :v0.3.0-cu124 / -cu128 tags to wire Step 5.1. Your remote video gen should then work end-to-end (ffmpeg present).

Resolving the thread. Thanks for the pre-build flag — saved a wasted ship.
